# Kaspar von Grünberg

> Kaspar von Grünberg is a founder and writer advancing Platform Engineering as the operating model for enterprise knowledge work. He is based near Berlin, Germany.

## Who he is

Kaspar von Grünberg helped establish Platform Engineering as a formal discipline. He writes independently on the theory that underpins platforms, and builds the ecosystem that brings it to practice.

Current focus: how platforms enable enterprise AI adoption.

## Organizations founded

- [Canyon](https://getcanyon.ai/) — AI base platform for enterprise. Semantic data layer for agentic applications.
- [PlatformCon](https://platformcon.com/) — The annual Platform Engineering conference. Largest event in the discipline.
- [PlatformEngineering.org](https://platformengineering.org/) — Open community. Largest Platform Engineering community globally.
- [Weave Intelligence](https://weaveintelligence.io/) — Analyst firm for Platform Engineering.
- [Platform University](https://university.platformengineering.org/) — Education programs for platform teams.
- [Humanitec](https://humanitec.com/) — Platform orchestrator. Reference implementation for Internal Developer Platforms.

## Writing

Essays and articles: [kasparvongruenberg.com/writing.html](https://kasparvongruenberg.com/writing.html)

Selected essays:
- "Why platform engineering will eat the world" — foundational thesis on platform engineering as the inevitable evolution of software development
- "Why every Internal Developer Platform needs a backend" — the distinction between frontend tooling and systematic platform architecture
- "Dark Side of Self-Service" — how cognitive load and abstraction failures undermine self-service rhetoric
- "Evaluating DevOps Maturity" — a framework for maturity benchmarking

## Book

Title: **Platform Engineering as an Operating Model for Knowledge Work**

A three-part book separating platform theory from vendor practice:
1. Origins — why platforms must be treated as products
2. Model — the Paths to Outcome conceptual framework
3. Practice — how to build and scale platforms inside enterprises

Expected publication: June 2026

## Newsletter

Periodic essays on Platform Engineering and enterprise AI adoption. No sponsors. No paid placements.

Published on Substack: [kasparvongruenberg.substack.com](https://kasparvongruenberg.substack.com/)

Topics:
- Platform Engineering as the foundation for enterprise AI
- Agentic development and the changing role of software engineers
- Business users as platform consumers
- Organizational design for knowledge work
- Field notes from enterprise roll-outs

## Notable interviews

- Barry O'Reilly, Unlearn — on building developer platforms, mentorship, and elite performance
- Kelsey Hightower, PlatformCon — on the origins, lessons, and future of platform engineering
- Ganesh Datta, Braintrust by Cortex — on why software production must move beyond artisanal execution
- Ivan Krnić, 0800-DEVOPS — on platform engineering as controlled, value-creating systems design

## Key concepts

**Internal Developer Platform (IDP)** — A self-service layer that abstracts infrastructure complexity and standardizes software delivery across an organization.

**Paths to Outcome** — Kaspar's conceptual framework describing how platforms should structure the relationship between platform teams and their consumers. Core to the book.

**Cost of Chaos** — A framework for measuring the organizational cost of inconsistent, undisciplined software delivery.

**Platform Engineering as operating model** — The thesis that Platform Engineering is not a tooling discipline but an organizational operating model for knowledge work.
